sections_processed: Article V: Cash Value and Loans
Article VIII: Estate Tax Planning Provisions

facts.fact: The insurer is Estate Planning Life Assurance Ltd.
The policy number is EPL-JSL-2024-1156.
The issue date is August 20, 2024.
The policy form is Second-to-Die Whole Life.
The first insured is William Alexander Hartford.
William Alexander Hartford's date of birth is December 5, 1956.
William Alexander Hartford's age at issue is 67.
William Alexander Hartford's sex is Male.
William Alexander Hartford's risk class is Standard.
The second insured is Elizabeth Anne Hartford.
Elizabeth Anne Hartford's date of birth is March 18, 1958.
Elizabeth Anne Hartford's age at issue is 66.
Elizabeth Anne Hartford's sex is Female.
Elizabeth Anne Hartford's risk class is Preferred.
The policy owner is The Hartford Family Irrevocable Life Insurance Trust, dated August 1, 2024.
The trustee of the Hartford Family Irrevocable Life Insurance Trust is First National Bank of Connecticut.
The death benefit is $2,500,000.
The annual premium is $32,400, guaranteed level for 10 years.
The premium payor is Hartford Family Irrevocable Trust.
The beneficiary is Hartford Family Irrevocable Life Insurance Trust.
This is a joint life insurance policy insuring two lives.
The death benefit is payable only upon the death of the SECOND insured to die (survivorship basis).
If the first insured dies, no death benefit is paid and the policy continues in force covering the surviving insured.
The death benefit is paid only after both insureds have died.
The purpose of this policy is estate planning and estate tax liquidity.
Upon the death of the second insured, the insurer will pay $2,500,000 to the designated beneficiary, minus any outstanding loans.
Proof of both deaths must be submitted: death certificate of first insured, death certificate of second insured, and completed claim forms.
Payment will be made within 30 days of receiving complete claim documentation.
When the first insured dies, no death benefit is paid, but the policy continues in force.
When the first insured dies, premium payment obligation continues.
When the first insured dies, the policy owner receives written confirmation.
The death certificate of the first insured must be filed with the insurer upon first death.
The annual premium of $32,400 is guaranteed level for the first 10 policy years.
After Year 10, the premium may increase but will never exceed the maximum guaranteed premium of $48,600 per year.
Premiums are payable annually on August 20th.
Premiums continue after the first death at the same rate.
Premium waiver is not available on joint survivorship policies.
The grace period is 60 days; if payment is not received, the policy cancels.
This policy accumulates cash value on a guaranteed schedule.
The projected cash value at end of Year 10 is $285,000 (based on current assumptions).
The projected cash value at end of Year 20 is $712,000 (based on current assumptions).
Policy loans are available after the policy has been in force for 2 years.
The maximum policy loan is 90% of cash surrender value.
The policy loan interest rate is variable, currently 5.5% per annum.
Loans reduce the death benefit dollar-for-dollar.
Policy loans are available before or after first death.
Partial withdrawals are not permitted under this policy type.
Upon the first death, the policy owner may elect to split this policy into two separate single-life policies.
The split option must be elected within 2 years of first death.
Each split policy's face amount is determined by actuarial allocation.
Evidence of insurability may be required for the split option.
If either insured commits suicide within 2 years of the issue date, the death benefit is limited to return of premiums paid.
The suicide clause resets if the policy is reinstated.
The insurer may contest this policy for material misrepresentation within 2 years of issue while both insureds are living.
No war exclusion applies to this policy.
No aviation exclusion applies to this policy.
No travel exclusions apply to this policy.
This policy is owned by an irrevocable trust to exclude proceeds from the insureds' taxable estates.
Proceeds paid to the trust should not be includable in either insured's estate if the trust is properly structured.
Estate planning considerations include: the three-year lookback rule if the policy was transferred to trust, Crummey power requirements, and annual exclusion gift planning.
If both insureds die in the same accident and it cannot be determined who died first, the death benefit will be paid immediately.
If deaths occur within 30 days of each other, they will be treated as simultaneous for claim purposes.
The policy owner (the Trust) has the right to designate and change beneficiaries.
The policy owner (the Trust) has the right to assign the policy.
The policy owner (the Trust) has the right to take policy loans.
The policy owner (the Trust) has the right to surrender the policy.
Neither insured has ownership rights unless specified in the trust document.
The policy may not be assigned without trustee authorization.
The policy owner may cancel within 30 days of policy receipt for a full refund of premium paid.
Estate Planning Life Assurance Ltd. is located in Hartford, Connecticut.
The authorized signatory is the Chairman & Chief Executive Officer of Estate Planning Life Assurance Ltd.
The policy was signed on August 20, 2024.
